entries.title: US to cut troop levels in Germany by 5,000 amid Trump spat with Merz
entries.snippet: The decision to reduce the US deployment to Germany comes amid a row between the two allies over Iran.

entries.title: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
entries.snippet: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.

entries.title: Thirteen killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, health ministry says
entries.snippet: Four women and a child are among the dead, as fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah continues despite a ceasefire.

entries.title: Spirit Airlines shutting down after rescue talks collapse
entries.snippet: The airline had been in talks with the Trump administration about a $500m bailout.

entries.title: 'If we sleep, they bite': Rats and weasels infest camps for displaced Gazans
entries.snippet: In the Gaza Strip, the daily battles are now with rats, weasels, and other pests spreading diseases.

entries.title: Who shot a Secret Service officer at the Trump press dinner?
entries.snippet: Court papers do not explicitly accuse the suspect of shooting the officer, even as officials say it was not "friendly fire".

entries.title: Ex-F1 driver and Paralympic champion Zanardi dies
entries.snippet: Former Formula 1 driver and Paralympic champion Alex Zanardi dies at the age of 59.

entries.title: Trump claims other presidents flouted war powers law. It's a mixed record
entries.snippet: Both Bushes and Reagan won authorisation for wars, but Obama and Clinton ducked the requirement.

entries.title: Billions of meals at risk due to Iran war, says fertiliser boss
entries.snippet: A shortage of fertiliser due to the Iran conflict could reduce crop yields and push prices higher, says the boss of Yara.

entries.title: US court limits mail-order access to abortion pill mifepristone
entries.snippet: The court decision curbs access to the drug used in medication abortions, which is currently the most common method for the procedure in the US.

entries.title: War criminal Mladic close to death, say lawyers asking judge for jail release
entries.snippet: Mladic, 84, was jailed for life for genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity during the Bosnian war in 1992-95.

entries.title: Missing Oscar found after Academy Award winner blocked from taking it on flight
entries.snippet: The airline said the Academy Award was safely in its care in Frankfurt and was arranging its return.

entries.title: Turkish police arrest more than 500 people at May Day rallies
entries.snippet: Turkey sees significant police deployments on 1 May each year, when marches led by workers and unions are held.

entries.title: Israeli police arrest man after nun attacked in Jerusalem
entries.snippet: The French nun was pushed over and kicked in the apparently unprovoked assault, amid a rise in harassment of Christians in the city by Jewish extremists.

entries.title: Israel releases all but two activists in Greece after intercepting Gaza aid flotilla
entries.snippet: The Global Sumud Flotilla denounced the interception as "piracy", while Israel's foreign ministry called the flotilla a "PR stunt".

entries.title: Zayn Malik cancels US tour and cuts UK dates after hospital bed photo shared
entries.snippet: The former One Direction singer has taken to Instagram to update fans as he cuts his world tour dates

entries.title: Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, military says
entries.snippet: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

entries.title: British couple jailed in Iran: 'We're likely to be here for a long time'
entries.snippet: Lindsay and Craig Foreman are facing the reality of a 10-year prison sentence following their arrest in Iran while on a motorcycle tour last year.

entries.title: Brazil's Congress approves plan to drastically cut Bolsonaro's jail term
entries.snippet: Last year ex-president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to 27 years in jail for plotting a coup after losing an election.

entries.title: Violence in Australian town after arrest of man over girl's murder
entries.snippet: Unrest took place outside hospital where man suspected of murdering a five-year-old girl was being treated.

entries.title: Opinion: The everyday tragedy of gun violence
entries.snippet: The White House Correspondents Association Dinner was one of several incidents of gun violence in the U.S. last week. Others ended in injuries and fatalities.

entries.title: Can Trump's latest pick for surgeon general make it through confirmation?
entries.snippet: Nicole Saphier, a breast cancer radiologist, is the president's third nominee for surgeon general. Will she get confirmed?

entries.title: Americans aren't sleeping enough. Here's what could help
entries.snippet: Nearly a third of Americans get less than the recommended seven hours of sleep a night. A lot of us struggle to get to bed as we power through tasks or get lost in endless scrolling. Here's help.

entries.title: New Netflix documentary reexamines Winnie Mandela's divisive legacy
entries.snippet: Winnie Madikizela-Mandela is one of the most revered — and controversial — women in South African history. In a new documentary her granddaughters examine the liberation icon in all her complexity.

entries.title: After Assad's fall, Syria's Kurds are left in limbo, feeling abandoned by the U.S.
entries.snippet: Caught in limbo after the fall of Syria's Bashar al-Assad, Kurdish families struggle with cold, loss and uncertainty — feeling abandoned by the U.S. allies they once fought alongside.

entries.title: 2026 Senate races to watch: From most likely to flip to Democratic long shots
entries.snippet: Democrats could have a narrow path to winning the Senate, with these specific states on the table.

entries.title: Spirit Airlines ceases operations after escalating financial struggles
entries.snippet: The low-cost carrier, which had been struggling for years, announced it will cease operations. Spirit had been seeking a $500 million lifeline from the White House, but talks failed to yield a deal.

entries.title: Air Force says former Qatari 747 will be ready to fly as Air Force One this summer
entries.snippet: The U.S. Air Force has finished modifying and testing a Boeing 747 jet donated by Qatar for temporary use as Air Force One and expects to have it ready for President Trump to use this summer.

entries.title: U.S. to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany in next 6-12 months
entries.snippet: The United States will withdraw about 5,000 troops from Germany, the Pentagon said Friday, fulfilling President Donald Trump's threat as he clashes with the German leader over the U.S. war with Iran.

entries.title: Wyoming celebrates 'nuclear renaissance' as feds approve license for a new reactor
entries.snippet: Construction of an advanced nuclear power plant partly funded by the U.S. government is now underway in Wyoming. The Bill Gates-backed company says its technology is proven but there are still hurdles to nuclear.

entries.title: Trump rejects Iran's latest proposal as he reviews new military options to relaunch war
entries.snippet: It has been 60 days since the Iran war began, which means Friday is a legal deadline for the Trump administration to seek congressional authorization. But the White House informed Congress that it didn't need approval because the war had been "terminated" during the current cease

entries.title: New video of Correspondents' Dinner shooting raises questions about presidential security
entries.snippet: The Justice Department has released video showing the moment an armed man stormed past security at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. The incident is raising serious questions about security surrounding the president at high-profile public events. Geoff Bennett speaks with J

entries.title: News Wrap: Trump announces 25% tariffs on cars and trucks from the EU
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Friday, Trump says he's raising tariffs next week on cars and trucks from the EU, the Pentagon says it has made deals with seven major tech companies to use their AI tools, a former Miami congressman with ties to Rubio was convicted of secretly lobbying for Venez

entries.title: Which states to watch as the battle for Senate control heats up ahead of 2026 midterms
entries.snippet: A total of 35 Senate seats will be on the ballot this November, with only 11 rated as remotely competitive by the Cook Political Report with Amy Walter. Republicans are on defense in more of those seats than Democrats, but Democrats need a longshot sweep to take control of the ch

entries.title: Brooks and Capehart on fallout from the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
entries.snippet: David Brooks of The Atlantic and Jonathan Capehart of MS NOW join Amna Nawaz to discuss the week in politics, including fallout from the third alleged assassination attempt on Trump, another indictment of a former FBI director and a consequential Supreme Court ruling.

entries.title: Palestinian and Israeli writers reflect on bridging divides in 'The Future is Peace'
entries.snippet: The Oct. 7 terrorist attack in Israel, and the war in Gaza that followed, have left countless families grappling with profound loss. Two men, one Israeli, the other Palestinian, tell the story of their unexpected journey toward understanding and reconciliation in a new book, "The

entries.title: New PBS series explores the often-overlooked history of American Muslims
entries.snippet: Starting this weekend, a new series on PBS dives into the early history of Muslims in the United States. The six-part documentary, "American Muslims: A History Revealed," looks at notable figures and communities spanning more than 200 years. Geoff Bennett speaks with Asma Khalid

entries.title: Court restricts abortion access across U.S. by blocking the mailing of mifepristone
entries.snippet: A panel of the New Orleans-based 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals is requiring that the abortion pill be distributed only in-person at clinics.

entries.title: Trump says a 'final proposal' to rescue Spirit Airlines is under consideration
entries.snippet: Speaking to reporters before departing the White House for Florida, Trump emphasized that a deal to rescue the financially strapped airline remained under review. The president did not provide details but said an announcement could come as soon as later Friday or Saturday.

entries.title: Pope Leo names priest who criticized Trump's immigration crackdown as West Virginia bishop
entries.snippet: The next bishop for West Virginia Catholics will be an El Salvador-born advocate for immigrants who has opposed U.S. President Donald Trump's immigration crackdown policies.

entries.title: Trump 'not satisfied' with Iran's proposal to end the war
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump rejected Iran's latest proposal to end the war between the countries, saying Friday he still was not satisfied while blaming Iran's "fractured" leadership.

entries.title: Trump says deadline for Congress to approve Iran war doesn't apply, claiming hostilities have 'terminated'
entries.snippet: Yet President Donald Trump also made it clear in a letter to Congress that the war may be far from over.

entries.title: WATCH: Trump says he raised tariffs on autos from EU to 25%
entries.snippet: Trump said in a social media post that the EU "is not complying with our fully agreed to Trade Deal," though he did not flesh out his objections in the post.

entries.title: WATCH: Correspondents' dinner shooting suspect seen running through security in surveillance footage
entries.snippet: Federal prosecutors released a video Thursday showing the moment authorities say a man armed with guns and knives tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner and attempt to kill President Donald Trump.

entries.title: Former congressman David Rivera convicted in case on secret Venezuela lobbying
entries.snippet: Jurors found Rivera and an associate guilty on all counts, including failing to register as a foreign agent with the Justice Department and conspiracy to commit money laundering as part of their work for former President Nicolás Maduro's government.

entries.title: What to know about May Day as workers face rising costs due to Iran war
entries.snippet: Activists worldwide gathered for May Day rallies and street protests Friday, calling for peace, higher wages and better working conditions as many workers grapple with rising energy costs and shrinking purchasing power tied to the Iran war.

entries.title: Man jailed after being charged with attempted murder in stabbings of Jewish men in London
entries.snippet: A 45-year-old man was charged Friday with attempted murder in the stabbings of two Jewish men in London, the latest in a string of attacks that have sparked fear and anger in Britain's Jewish community.

entries.title: Profit for the biggest U.S. oil companies declined in 1st quarter – but only on paper
entries.snippet: With energy prices depressed at the start of the year, Exxon Mobil and Chevron had arranged hedges to offset volatility, a standard practice in the industry.

entries.title: 4 takeaways from Hegseth's hearings on historic defense budget request, Iran war
entries.snippet: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth appeared before Congress at a pair of hourslong hearings this week for the first time since the Trump administration went to war against Iran, with the Pentagon chief facing tough questions from skeptical Democrats.

entries.title: What to know after House passes Homeland Security funding and ends historic shutdown
entries.snippet: The longest shutdown of a U.S. government agency in history has ended, after Trump signed a House-passed bill to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security. The administration had warned that money to pay thousands of federal security workers would run out after Thursday wi

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Alex Zanardi, F1 driver and multiple CART and Paralympic champion, dies at 59
entries.snippet: Italian race driver turned Paralympian Alex Zanardi has died aged 59. The former Formula 1 driver and two-time CART champion lost his legs in a horrific crash at the peak of his powers, but his drive to compete endured.

entries.title: Germany news: US troop pullout 'expected' — Pistorius
entries.snippet: Defense Minister Boris Pistorius says Europe must do more for its own security after the US withdrawal announcement. Police have said some violence marred mostly peaceful May Day demos in Berlin. DW has more.

entries.title: Iran: Jailed Nobel winner Narges Mohammadi hospitalized
entries.snippet: Iranian activist Narges Mohammadi is in hospital after suffering a "cardiac crisis" in prison, her family says. Mohammadi won the 2023 Nobel Peace Prize for her advocacy of women's rights and freedom.

entries.title: How the Iran war is hurting travelers, airline industry
entries.snippet: Europeans may have to reconsider their summer travel plans, as a jet fuel shortage triggered by the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz could lead airlines to set higher ticket prices.

entries.title: US to withdraw thousands of troops from Germany
entries.snippet: The decision to cut US troops comes amid rising tensions after German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the US was being "humiliated" by Iran's leadership.

entries.title: Kosovo to hold snap elections in June
entries.snippet: Kosovo will soon hold its third parliamentary election in a year and a half. Prime Minister Albin Kurti was able to form a government in February, but now there's political deadlock over who should be elected president.

entries.title: Humpback whale Timmy released in North Sea but risk remains
entries.snippet: After weeks of drama, the stranded humpback whale is back in his natural habitat, but still not out of harm's way.

entries.title: The significance of US military bases in Germany
entries.snippet: President Trump is planning to cut US troop numbers in Germany. How many US bases are there in the country and what's their strategic importance?

entries.title: Spirit Airlines says they are going out of business
entries.snippet: All flights by the troubled US carrier Spirit Airlines have been cancelled, with the company saying it was ending operations "immediately" after talks on a government bailout faltered.

entries.title: New Oscars rules exclude AI performers, require scripts written by human
entries.snippet: AI performers will not qualify for Oscars under new Academy rules that also overhaul the international film category and acting nominations.

entries.title: Can Iran withstand the US naval blockade?
entries.snippet: The Trump administration is trying to force Iran into concessions by cutting off its oil exports. Will it prevail?

entries.title: Ukraine plans army pay hikes, phased discharge — Zelenskyy
entries.snippet: The Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced reforms to boost morale and address military complaints about exhaustion and uneven workloads on the frontline.

entries.title: Trump to raise tariffs on cars, trucks from EU to 25%
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump said he's raising tariffs on cars and trucks from the European Union to 25%, claiming the bloc was not complying with its trade agreement with the US.

entries.title: Germany: Lufthansa apologizes for lost Oscar statuette
entries.snippet: Lufthansa said it would conduct "an internal review" to find out how the Oscar statuette belonging to Russian director Pavel Talankin went missing in Frankfurt.

entries.title: May Day marked by protests, celebrations, arrests
entries.snippet: Labor Day comes as rising energy costs and shrinking purchasing triggered by the Iran war has hurt workers around the world.

entries.title: Pentagon inks deals with AI giants, but not Anthropic
entries.snippet: The pacts enable OpenAI, Google and SpaceX to bring top-tier artificial intelligence (AI) models onto the US military’s secure classified networks.

entries.title: Germany needs no tips from Donald Trump, says Merz's deputy
entries.snippet: Germany's vice chancellor says US President Donald Trump has made a mess of the Iran war and that his focus should be on ending it. As fuel prices soar, Lars Klingbeil said Trump bears responsibility for the fallout.

entries.title: Is Myanmar's military winning the civil war?
entries.snippet: After five years of war, armed resistance groups still challenge Myanmar's military across much of the country. But the generals are now stemming losses and reclaiming some territory.

entries.title: Iran war expands press crackdown, leaving information vacuum
entries.snippet: For many journalists in Iran, the truth is increasingly being treated as a security threat. Authorities are tightening an already repressive reporting environment, where information is strictly controlled.
